text: Let's not {{c1::get bogged down in}} the implementation details during this high-level sync.
explanation: get bogged down in means to become so stuck or overwhelmed by small details that you lose sight of the main goal and stop making progress.
usage:
Pattern: get bogged down in [details/logistics/work]
  • Don't get bogged down in minor edits; focus on completing the draft.
  • Our discussion got bogged down in budget arguments.
spanish: No nos empantanemos en los detalles de implementación durante esta sincronización de alto nivel.

text: Welcome everyone; let's start by reviewing the agenda for today's call and identifying our {{c1::key objectives}}.
explanation: 'Key objectives' are the primary, most important goals or outcomes that a meeting or project is designed to achieve.
usage:
Pattern: key objectives / main goals
  • The key objectives of this phase are database cleanup and indexing.
  • What are the key objectives for today's meeting?
spanish: Bienvenidos todos; comencemos revisando la agenda para la llamada de hoy e identificando nuestros objetivos clave.

text: I'll be facilitating the discussion today, and I want to make sure everyone has an opportunity to {{c1::voice their opinion}}.
explanation: To 'voice your opinion' means to express your thoughts, beliefs, or views on a subject openly and publicly.
usage:
Pattern: voice opinion / express views
  • Employees are encouraged to voice their opinions in team surveys.
  • He was not afraid to voice his opinion during the board meeting.
spanish: Estaré moderando la discusión hoy, y quiero asegurarme de que todos tengan la oportunidad de expresar su opinión.

text: Let's keep an eye on the time: we need to move on to the next topic on the agenda in {{c1::five minutes}}.
explanation: To 'keep an eye on the time' means to monitor the clock continuously to ensure the meeting doesn't run late.
usage:
Pattern: keep an eye on the time / track time
  • We must keep an eye on the time to avoid missing our flight.
  • I am keeping an eye on the time so we finish before noon.
spanish: Estemos atentos al tiempo: necesitamos pasar al siguiente tema de la agenda en cinco minutos.

text: Could you please keep your updates concise so we have enough time to discuss the new {{c1::marketing campaign}}?
explanation: A 'marketing campaign' is an organized, strategic course of action to promote a product, service, or brand across media channels.
usage:
Pattern: marketing campaign / advertising campaign
  • We are launching a new marketing campaign next week.
  • The marketing campaign resulted in a 30% increase in traffic.
spanish: ¿Podría por favor mantener sus actualizaciones breves para que tengamos suficiente tiempo para discutir la nueva campaña de marketing?

text: Let's summarize the main points we've agreed on before we adjourn the meeting for {{c1::today}}.
explanation: To 'adjourn the meeting' is formal language meaning to temporarily end or suspend a meeting or assembly.
usage:
Pattern: adjourn the meeting / end the session
  • The committee decided to adjourn the meeting until next Tuesday.
  • The chairman adjourned the meeting at 4:00 PM.
spanish: Resumamos los puntos principales en los que hemos estado de acuerdo antes de levantar la sesión por hoy.

text: If there are no objections, let's approve the previous meeting minutes and move to the {{c1::first item}}.
explanation: To 'approve the minutes' is the formal process in business meetings of verifying that the written record of the previous meeting is accurate.
usage:
Pattern: approve the minutes / review minutes
  • The board voted to approve the minutes of the January meeting.
  • First, we must approve the minutes before discussing new business.
spanish: Si no hay objeciones, aprobemos la minuta de la reunión anterior y pasemos al primer punto.

text: I'd like to invite our guest speaker to share their insights on industry trends and customer {{c1::behavior}}.
explanation: To 'share insights' means to explain deep understandings, professional knowledge, or analytical conclusions about a topic to others.
usage:
Pattern: share insights / offer recommendations
  • The analyst shared valuable insights on market growth.
  • Thank you for sharing your insights with us today.
spanish: Me gustaría invitar a nuestro orador invitado a compartir sus conocimientos/perspectivas sobre las tendencias de la industria y el comportamiento del cliente.

text: Let's stay focused on the topic at hand; we can discuss other issues during our next scheduled {{c1::sync call}}.
explanation: The 'topic at hand' is the specific subject, problem, or agenda item currently being discussed or addressed.
usage:
Pattern: topic at hand / current subject
  • Please limit your questions to the topic at hand.
  • The manager guided the team back to the topic at hand.
spanish: Mantengámonos enfocados en el tema en cuestión; podemos discutir otros asuntos durante nuestra próxima llamada de sincronización programada.

text: Thank you for your active participation today; I will send out a follow-up email with the next steps {{c1::shortly}}.
explanation: 'Active participation' refers to actively engaging, speaking, and contributing value during a group meeting or workshop.
usage:
Pattern: active participation / engage in discussion
  • The success of the brainstorming session depended on everyone's active participation.
  • Thank you all for your active participation today.
spanish: Gracias por su participación activa hoy; enviaré un correo electrónico de seguimiento con los siguientes pasos en breve.

text: Let's start the meeting on time; we have a lot of topics to cover on our agenda {{c1::today}}.
explanation: 'Today' refers to the present day, commonly used to set context at the start of a business call.
usage:
Pattern: on our agenda today / schedule for today
  • What is the main topic on our agenda today?
  • We completed all tasks scheduled for today.
spanish: Comencemos la reunión a tiempo; tenemos muchos temas que cubrir en nuestra agenda hoy.

text: I'll be leading the meeting today; please let me know if anyone has any updates to add to the {{c1::agenda}}.
explanation: An 'agenda' is a list of items or topics to be discussed or resolved during a formal meeting.
usage:
Pattern: meeting agenda / items on the agenda
  • Please review the agenda before the call starts.
  • The manager added two new points to the meeting agenda.
spanish: Estaré guiando la reunión hoy; por favor, avísenme si alguien tiene alguna actualización que agregar a la agenda.

text: Let's keep our updates brief so we have enough time to discuss the database migration {{c1::plan}}.
explanation: A 'plan' is a detailed proposal for doing or achieving something, here referencing the technical migration plan.
usage:
Pattern: migration plan / project plan
  • The architect drafted a detailed migration plan.
  • We must follow the rollout plan to avoid service disruption.
spanish: Mantengamos nuestras actualizaciones breves para que tengamos suficiente tiempo para discutir el plan de migración de la base de datos.

text: Could you please summarize your findings in two sentences to help us stay on {{c1::schedule}}?
explanation: To 'stay on schedule' is the business collocation meaning to complete tasks or run meetings within the planned timetable.
usage:
Pattern: stay on schedule / keep on schedule
  • We adjusted the timeline to ensure we stay on schedule.
  • The project manager is responsible for keeping the team on schedule.
spanish: ¿Podrías por favor resumir tus hallazgos en dos oraciones para ayudarnos a mantenernos según lo programado?

text: Let's review the action items we agreed on before we close the meeting for {{c1::this afternoon}}?
explanation: 'This afternoon' refers to the latter part of the present day, commonly set as a deadline or closure time for calls.
usage:
Pattern: this afternoon / late afternoon
  • The migration is scheduled for this afternoon.
  • I will send the report this afternoon.
spanish: ¿Revisamos las tareas pendientes en las que estuvimos de acuerdo antes de cerrar la reunión de esta tarde?

text: If there are no objections, let's approve the previous meeting minutes and move to our first {{c1::topic}}.
explanation: A 'topic' is a subject or theme of discussion or study in a meeting.
usage:
Pattern: first topic / main topic / subject of discussion
  • The first topic on our agenda is database latency.
  • Let's move to the next topic.
spanish: Si no hay objeciones, aprobemos la minuta de la reunión anterior y pasemos a nuestro primer tema.

text: I'd like to invite our product manager to present the new features scheduled for the next product {{c1::release}}.
explanation: A 'release' (or deployment) is the distribution of a new version of software to production.
usage:
Pattern: product release / next release
  • We are coding the features for the next release.
  • The code is scheduled for the upcoming release.
spanish: Me gustaría invitar a nuestro gerente de producto a presentar las nuevas características programadas para el próximo lanzamiento del producto.

text: Let's stay focused on the project milestones; we can discuss other issues in our weekly {{c1::check-in call}}.
explanation: A 'check-in call' (or status sync) is a regular, brief meeting to monitor progress and coordinate tasks.
usage:
Pattern: weekly check-in call / daily check-in
  • The manager schedules a check-in call every Monday.
  • Let's use the check-in call to coordinate our updates.
spanish: Mantengámonos enfocados en los hitos del proyecto; podemos discutir otros asuntos en nuestra llamada de control semanal.

text: Thank you all for joining; I will send out a follow-up email with the meeting notes and action items {{c1::shortly}}.
explanation: 'Shortly' is an adverb meaning in a short time from now; soon, indicating rapid follow-up action.
usage:
Pattern: shortly after / shortly before / arrive shortly
  • The service was restored shortly after the reboot.
  • The manager will join the call shortly.
spanish: Gracias a todos por unirse; enviaré un correo electrónico de seguimiento con las notas de la reunión y las tareas pendientes en breve.

text: If anyone has any remaining questions, please post them on our Slack channel to avoid running {{c1::over time}}.
explanation: To run 'over time' (or exceed schedule) is the phrasal verb meaning to take longer than the allocated meeting duration.
usage:
Pattern: running over time / exceed time limits
  • We must end the call now to avoid running over time.
  • The workshop ran over time by thirty minutes.
spanish: Si alguien tiene alguna pregunta restante, por favor publíquela en nuestro canal de Slack para evitar pasarnos de tiempo.
